commands.repair_reason: Needs contributor code changes plus after-fix real behavior proof before merge; automation should not replace the author's live performance proof for this external PR.; Needs attention: The diff does not add dependencies or CI changes, but the fallback can keep stale plugin runtime state across security-relevant plugin config, trust, or load-path changes.; Review finding: [P2] Preserve load-options compatibility on... (sha=ddd566fbf6a717df0536c60789214cbb95480932)
commands.review_summary:
The PR adds a standalone runtime loader fallback that reuses an active registry after a strict cache-key miss when workspace and required plugin IDs match, plus regression tests and a changelog entry.

Reproducibility: yes. source-level. The linked issue gives concrete gateway/fresh-dispatch steps, and current main still shows dispatch calling the standalone runtime loader with lean options that can miss strict compatibility and fall through to loadOpenClawPlugins; I did not run the live heap profile.
commands.review_followup: Needs contributor code changes plus after-fix real behavior proof before merge; automation should not replace the author's live performance proof for this external PR.

target.title: perf(plugins): reuse active registry on dispatch when cache keys diverge
